description: Figure 1 comprises two panels illustrating the heat treatment protocol and kinetic analysis for low-density nano-bainitic Al-C steels. Panel (a) presents schematic temperature-time profiles for five steel grades (S1–S5). Samples were austenitized at varying temperatures (T_A): S1, S2, and S5 at 1150 °C for 15 min, S3 at 1220 °C for 20 min, and S4 at either 1150 °C or 1250 °C for 15 min. Following heating at 10 K s⁻¹, specimens were rapidly cooled at 100 K s⁻¹ to specific austempering temperatures (T_B) above the martensite start temperature (M_s): S1 and S2 to 300 °C or 350 °C, S3 to 270 °C or 350 °C, S4 to 320 °C or 350 °C (depending on T_A), and S5 to 335 °C or 365 °C. Panel (b) displays a representative dilatometric analysis showing relative length change (RLC, %) and transformation rate (s⁻¹) versus time. The bainitic transformation finish time (B_t) is determined as the point where the transformation rate decreases to a negligible level, corresponding to the onset of the plateau in the RLC curve (approximately 1500 s). This methodology enables precise determination of isothermal holding durations required for near-complete nano-bainitic transformation across the studied alloy systems.
